used_to_decide: Phase 1 exhaustive pass on ROUND_4 days 1–3; K=5/20/100 forward price ticks; aggressor vs BBO; spread regime + session buckets; bursts; graph + 2-hop motifs; baseline residuals vs (symbol,spr_regime). Ready for Phase 2.

bullets.conclusion: Aggressive buys: Mark 67 shows large positive mean forward same-symbol markout at K=5/20/100 (n=164) but is a thin slice vs dominant flow. Mark 55 buys mildly positive short-horizon; Mark 38 buys negative at K=20 on same symbol. Aggressive sells from Mark 22 show negative short-horizon option markouts (consistent with passive adverse selection against the basket seller). All patterns need sim PnL before trading.

Saturated (buyer,seller,symbol)-only cell means make residuals ~0 by construction; refined baseline uses pooled mean U mark@20 by (symbol, spr_regime). Large residuals concentrate in tight-book extract prints (e.g. Mark 55→Mark 01 vs baseline) and Mark 01→Mark 22 on VEV_5300 in tight regime (see r4_pair_01_22_5300_by_regime.txt).

Dominant edge Mark 01→Mark 22 (1339 prints); hydro duopoly Mark 14↔Mark 38 reciprocal. Two-hop motif mass is repetitive Mark01/Mark22 ladder (725 chains in 5k-ts window) — structural bot choreography, not episodic insider paths.
Same-timestamp bursts (≥4 rows): forward U mid change at K=20 ticks averages ~0.345 vs ~0.095 for non-burst rows (n≈1395 vs 2884) — strongest pooled tape-level conditioning signal in this pass.

Without full L2 history, proxy is markout after aggressor prints: Mark 22 as seller on options shows negative short-horizon same-symbol markouts (many prints) — candidate 'do not lean passive into Mark22 sells' rule for future MM sims.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Burst timestamps (≥4 prints at same day+timestamp) predict higher mean forward extract mid move at K=20 ticks vs non-burst control
Mark 01 → Mark 22 on VEV_5300 in tight spread regime shows strongly negative residual vs (symbol×spr_regime) baseline on U@20
Mark 55 aggressive buys vs Mark 01 on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT in tight regime: large negative residual vs symbol-regime baseline at K=20
Mark 67 aggressive buys (cross-section): large positive short-horizon same-symbol and extract markouts at K=5/20
Dominant graph hub Mark 01→Mark 22 with repetitive 2-hop ladders — structural scheduling candidate for event-triggered sizing (not directional alpha alone)
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: 1339 directed prints; 725 repeated 01→22→01→22 chains in short window

no_edge_or_negative: (buyer,seller,symbol)-only baseline residuals are degenerate (~0) — must condition on spread regime or product dynamics.
Mark 01→Mark 22 on VEV_5300 pooled (all regimes) does not show large unconditional U edge at K=20 (mean small positive); signal lives in regime splits.
Pure 2-hop motif counts alone do not map to extract direction without pairing to forward markout regression (left for Phase 2 sim).

used_to_decide: Phase 2 on ROUND_4 days 1-3: burst typing, microprice/spread, signed flow lead-lag, hydro-U lags, IV by Mark on VEV_5300, Mark22 burst stratification.

round4_phase2_complete.interaction_with_phase1: Phase-1 pooled burst extract edge refines: almost all bursts include Mark01->Mark22; pure 01->22-only bursts (n=194) mean U fwd20 ~0.33 vs control ~-0.14. Mixed bursts (n=98) have negative mean U fwd5 (-0.18) but positive fwd20 (0.29). Per-day burst mean U fwd20 is unstable (day1 -0.08, day2 +0.33, day3 +0.63), so Phase-1 burst headline is not day-stable at K=20 without extra conditioning.

bullets.conclusion: Typed bursts separate pure basket vs mixed; day table shows dispersion.
Signed extract flow: small contemporaneous corr, lag-1 ~0.074. Hydro vs lagged U diffs near zero.
Burst composition (pure vs mixed) is the main new split beyond Phase-1 spread buckets.
BBO IV on VEV_5300: Mark01->Mark22 vs other prints, medians ~0.246 both (n_other small).
Mark22 VEV sells: slightly less negative 20-tick markout inside bursts; sub-tick.
Not simulated in Phase 2; defer to trader once rules chosen.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Pure Mark01->Mark22-only bursts vs non-burst control on U fwd20
top_5_tradeable_edges.phase1_link: Confirms/refines Phase-1 burst extract signal with explicit basket typing.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: U spread predicts next-tick absolute U move (compression)
Signed aggressive extract flow leads dU by 1 tick
Mixed bursts vs pure 01->22 bursts on U fwd5
top_5_tradeable_edges.phase1_link: Shows not all bursts are equivalent; falsifies single burst bucket.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark22 VEV sells: burst vs non burst same-symbol 20-tick markout
top_5_tradeable_edges.phase1_link: Weak execution-side nuance for Mark22 in bursts.

phase1_falsifications_or_limits: Burst mean U fwd20 unstable by day; pooled Phase-1 burst stat masks heterogeneity.
VEV_5300 IV by Mark pair null in this slice.

used_to_decide: Phase 3 re-runs Sonic joint gate (5200 and 5300 spread <= 2) on R4 aligned panel; inclineGod spread-spread panels; highest-value Phase 1/2 stats (tape forward U, burst, Mark01->Mark22) conditioned on gate; document interaction vs ungated Phase 1/2.

round4_phase3_complete.comparison_to_phase1_phase2_without_gate: Phase 1 pooled burst vs non-burst on U@20: mean burst ~0.345 vs non ~0.095 (r4_phase1_summary). Phase 2 showed burst U@20 unstable by day and that pure 01->22 bursts still need context. With the joint gate: aligned-panel mean forward U@20 is ~0.324 when tight vs ~-0.121 when loose (diff ~0.445 ticks, n=29940 valid rows; P(tight)~0.257). So the gate does not remove the burst effect (tight+burst mean U@20 ~0.338 vs Phase-1 burst ~0.345; r4_phase3_compare_phase1_burst_vs_gate.json) but it stacks a strong tape-level conditioning signal Sonic described: loose-book rows have negative mean forward U at K=20 and especially K=100 (day 3 loose K=100 mean ~-1.77). Interpretation: gate mainly filters execution-noise regime rather than 'cleaning' burst identity; burst and tight are overlapping bot states.

bullets.topic: Sonic joint gate on forward extract (aligned timestamps)
bullets.conclusion: Pooled tight minus loose mean forward U: K=5 ~+0.105 ticks, K=20 ~+0.445, K=100 ~+1.95. By day at K=20: day1 tight +0.599 vs loose -0.048; day2 +0.464 vs -0.018; day3 +0.188 vs -0.395 (direction always favorable to tight; loose mean very negative on day 3).

bullets.topic: inclineGod spread-spread and spread vs extract
bullets.conclusion: Daily corr(5200 spread, 5300 spread) ~0.18-0.27. Corr of voucher spreads with extract spread is near zero; corr(s5200,m_u) and corr(s5300,m_u) are moderate positive (~0.32-0.47), consistent with co-movement of book width and level rather than a simple spread-spread extract chain in correlation alone.

Dominant pair almost always prints when the gate is tight (all symbols n_tight=1336 vs n_loose=3 at trade timestamps). Pooled Mark01->Mark22 on VEV_5300: mean U@20 ~+0.042 (n=132, all tight). By day on VEV_5300 U@20: volatile (day1 mean -1.67, day2 +1.02, day3 +0.40) so pair-specific edge is not day-stable at K=20 despite structural co-location with tight books.

bullets.topic: Burst x gate on trade-level U markout
bullets.conclusion: Inside tight gate, non-burst rows actually have higher mean U@20 (~0.431, n=769) than burst rows (~0.338, n=1389). Loose non-burst mean is slightly negative (~-0.027, n=2115). Gate stratifies levels more than burst does once tight.

bullets.topic: Phase 1 style participant alpha re-run under gate (aggressive buy/sell, min n=30)
bullets.conclusion: Mark 38 aggressive buys: loose mean U@20 -0.26 (n=531) vs tight +0.42 (n=200). Mark 38 aggressive sells: loose -0.51 vs tight +1.12 (n=190). Mark 67 remains strongly positive in loose (n=137) with no tight bucket at min_n. Mark 22 aggressive sells exist almost only when tight (n=1419, mean U@20 +0.271). Shows strong three-way interaction (Mark, aggressor, gate) but some cells need wider min_n in future sims.

ranked_edges.edge: Joint tight gate on aligned R4 panel: higher mean forward extract mid move at K in {5,20,100} vs loose
ranked_edges.vs_phase12: Stronger and more monotonic in K than any single counterparty slice in Phase 1; orthogonal to Phase-2 spread->next |dU| correlation which is a different horizon (1 row).

ranked_edges.edge: (Mark 38, aggressive buy, tight gate) vs loose on U@20
ranked_edges.vs_phase12: Phase 1 flagged Mark 38 buys negative at K=20 unconditionally; gate splits sign and magnitude.

ranked_edges.edge: (Mark 38, aggressive sell, tight gate) vs loose on U@20
ranked_edges.vs_phase12: Large interaction; not visible without gate conditioning.

ranked_edges.edge: Mark 01 -> Mark 22 co-located with tight gate (all symbols); pooled U@20 positive when tight
ranked_edges.effect: mean U@20 ~+0.295 when tight (n=1336); negligible loose sample
ranked_edges.vs_phase12: Confirms structural basket flow runs in tight voucher surface; loose prints are rare for this pair.

ranked_edges.edge: VEV_5200 vs VEV_5300 spread correlation by day (inclineGod)
ranked_edges.n: 10000 rows per day on aligned panel
ranked_edges.vs_phase12: Descriptive regime context for MM quoting; not a standalone PnL rule.

no_edge_or_negative: Mark 01 -> Mark 22 on VEV_5300 only: mean U@20 small positive pooled (~+0.042) and day-split unstable (r4_phase3_mark01_22_vev5300_mark20u_by_gate_day.csv).
Tight gate does not increase burst mean U@20 vs Phase-1 burst mean; non-burst under tight has higher mean than burst under tight.
Mark 67 tight bucket absent at min_n=30 for aggressive buys (thin tail).
round4_phase3_complete.primary_r4_thesis_candidate: Legitimate stacked signal: tape-level forward extract drift favors Sonic tight joint gate at multiple K; counterparty identity interacts (Mark 38 aggressor flips; Mark01->Mark22 structurally tight). Next step is simulator PnL (MM skew / fade rules) because markouts are not tradeable without execution model.

analysis_description: Post Phase 3 execution probe: trader_v2.py encodes gate + fair value + Mark22 post-fill widening (see trader docstring). Offline done for Phases 1-3; this records first live MM backtest in this strategy folder.
used_to_decide: Quantify whether a minimal counterparty rule (widen after buying VEV from Mark 22 tape seller) adds stability on top of v24-style gated MM; worse vs all gap is small (+2414), both positive across days 1-3.

backtest_summary.note: Counterparty branch uses own_trades from previous tick; market_trades at run() entry is empty so burst-at-timestamp gating is not implemented in sim.

analysis_description: Tape counts: Mark 22 as buyer vs seller on VEV prints (Round 4 days 1-3); motivates symmetric counterparty handling in trader_v3.
used_to_decide: Mark 22 is passive seller on ~76% of VEV prints (1433/1878); Mark 22 as VEV buyer only 6 prints (0.3%) all on day 3. Bidirectional MM adjustment after sell-to-M22-buyer is correct structurally but inert on this tape.

analysis_description: trader_v3 = v2 + widen after VEV sell fill to aggressive Mark 22 (prior tick). Backtest identical to v2 because tape rarely has M22 as VEV buyer.
used_to_decide: Falsifies expecting PnL lift from sell-side M22 rule on current R4 slice; keep rule for completeness or drop for simplicity.
